content.problemStatement:
Chef is building an Airport in Chefland.

There are NNN airplanes such that the ithi^{th}ith airplane arrives at the AiA_iAi​-th minute and departs at the DiD_iDi​-th minute.

Find the **minimum** number of runways required at the airport so that all the arrivals and departures can occur smoothly.

**Note:**


Arrival and departure timings are given in minutes after midnight.
A plane uses the runway only in the minute at which it arrives or departs. Between the arrival and departure times, the runway is available for use by other airplanes.
One runway can be used by only one airplane at a point in time.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of airplanes.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the arrival timings of ithi^{th}ith airplane.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers D1,D2,…,DND_1, D_2, \dots, D_ND1​,D2​,…,DN​ denoting the departure timings of ithi^{th}ith airplane.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** number of runways required at the airport.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 8*10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains integer N, the number of airplanes. Second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN, the arrival minutes. Third line contains N space‑separated integers D1 … DN, the departure minutes.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of runways required.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: At minute 1 two arrivals, at minute 2 two departures and one arrival, so maximum concurrent events = 3. Test case 2: Minute 4 has three events. Test case 3: No minute has more than one event.
All arrivals and departures occur at distinct minutes, therefore only one runway is ever needed.
Four arrivals at minute 1 and four departures at minute 2, so the peak load is 4.

solution.approach: Each airplane occupies a runway only at two discrete minutes: its arrival minute and its departure minute. Therefore the number of runways needed at any minute equals the total number of arrivals plus departures occurring at that minute. The answer for a test case is the maximum such count over all minutes from 0 to 1439. We can count events using an array cnt[1440] initialized to zero, increment cnt[Ai] and cnt[Di] for every airplane, and finally take the maximum value in cnt.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N + 1440) per test case, which is O(N) overall because 1440 is a constant.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1440) ≈ O(1) extra space per test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it)
        A = [next(it) for _ in range(N)]
        cnt = [0]*1440
        for i in range(N):
            d = next(it)
            cnt[A[i]] += 1
            cnt[d] += 1
        out_lines.append(str(max(cnt)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
